## Enzyme Extraction Summary: Rothlisberger 2008 Kemp Elimination Catalysts Dataset
### Dataset Overview
This analysis covers 27 unique Kemp eliminase variants across 5 scaffolds (1THF, 1A53, 1JCL, 1H61, 2RKX), derived from normalized variant data, with supporting text extraction from 3 replicate tables and vision extraction from 3 replicate plots of Figure 2a/b. All variants catalyze the conversion of 5-nitrobenzisoxazole to 2-cyano-4-nitrophenolate.
### Significant Improvements
The top performing variant is R7 10/11G (KE07 evolved round 7) with a catalytic efficiency of 2537 M⁻¹ s⁻¹, representing a ~208-fold improvement over the KE07 wildtype baseline (12.2 M⁻¹ s⁻¹). The second top variant KE07 Round 7 1/3H has a nearly equivalent efficiency of 2520 M⁻¹ s⁻¹ (~207-fold improvement). All top 5 variants are evolved from the 1THF scaffold, with efficiency improvements ranging from 23.8x to 208x relative to KE07 WT.
### Parameter Coverage
- Km: 55.6% (15/27 variants)
- kcat: 55.6% (15/27 variants)
- kcat/Km: 96.3% (26/27 variants)
- Tm: 0% (0/27 variants)
### Data Gaps
44.4% of variants lack reported Km and kcat values, and no variants have reported melting temperature (Tm) data. 17 variants have reported point mutations that conflict with the canonical scaffold sequence for the reported position, indicating potential sequence annotation inconsistencies. One variant (KE16 D48A) has no reported kinetic parameters at all.
